stories.headline: Denver officer shoots and kills man who fired at police near South Quebec Street
stories.summary:
A Denver Police officer fatally shot a man early Saturday morning after the man fired at officers at an apartment complex at 4363 S. Quebec St., near East Quincy Avenue and South Quebec Street. Officers had been dispatched shortly before 2 a.m. for a report of an armed confrontation in the parking lot, which police described as a possible carjacking. Officers located the suspect based on a description, approached him, and gave commands before the man fired one shot at them.

An officer returned fire, striking the man, who was transported to a hospital in critical condition. He was pronounced dead just before 12:30 p.m. Saturday, according to Denver Police. Denver Police Chief Ron Thomas delivered a statement via video shortly after the initial shooting.

The department did not immediately release the identities of the officer or the deceased man. The shooting will be subject to standard departmental review processes. The incident marks another officer-involved shooting in the Denver metro area this year.

stories.headline: Thornton woman found dead in home, police investigating as homicide
stories.summary:
Thornton police are investigating the death of a woman as a homicide after officers found her body inside a home on the 1900 block of West 101st Avenue on Friday morning. Officers were called to conduct a welfare check after the woman had not been seen or heard from in several days.

Detectives believe the woman was killed sometime between 9 p.m. Wednesday and Friday morning. No one else was inside the home when officers arrived, and no arrests have been announced. The woman's identity is expected to be released by the Adams County coroner's office.

Thorn Police said detectives are conducting interviews and pursuing leads. Anyone with information is asked to contact the Thornton Police Department tip line at 720-977-5069.

stories.headline: Metro Denver Uber driver arrested for sexually assaulting passenger, more victims suspected
stories.summary:
Arvada police announced the arrest of Javier Delgado-Cordoba, 29, on suspicion of sexually assaulting a female passenger while working as an Uber driver in January. According to investigators, the woman and her boyfriend requested a ride from a Denver-area bar to a home in Arvada. When the boyfriend briefly left the vehicle to unlock the home, Delgado-Cordoba entered the back seat and assaulted the woman before driving away with her still inside.

The woman was eventually dropped off at a business in another city, where employees helped her contact family. Delgado-Cordoba was identified through his ride-hail profile, social media and DNA evidence. He had driven for Uber for approximately one year, completing more than 1,000 rides, and may have used multiple vehicles, police said.

Delgado-Cordoba turned himself in Monday and is being held at the Jefferson County jail on $20,000 bail with a court appearance set for May 20. Arvada Police Chief Ed Brady said investigators believe there may be additional victims and encouraged anyone who believes they were affected to contact law enforcement or the department's tip line at 720-898-7171. The case renews scrutiny of a bill Gov. Jared Polis vetoed last year that would have imposed new safety requirements on ride-hail companies.

stories.headline: Denver metro average rent falls to 2022 levels as apartment oversupply drives concessions to record highs
stories.summary:
Average rents in the Denver metro dropped 3.4% over the past year to $1,758 per month in the first quarter of 2026, matching dollar-for-dollar the average rent recorded in the first quarter of 2022, according to a new report from the Apartment Association of Metro Denver. The decline is attributed primarily to a surge in new apartment construction, with more than 70,000 units added over the past five years, increasing the rental inventory by 18% to approximately 452,591 apartments.

Landlords have responded to the oversupply by offering record concessions, averaging $180 in monthly savings on new leases — equivalent to four to five weeks of free rent per year. That brings the effective average rent to roughly $1,580 per month. Denver's concession levels are the highest in the nation, according to Zillow data.

Industry leaders say the trend illustrates how increasing housing supply can improve affordability, though they note the current market dynamics differ from 2022, when rents were just beginning a sharp rise following pandemic-era stagnation. The rental market absorbed nearly 20,000 new apartments in 2024 and another 15,330 last year, roughly double the typical annual absorption rate of 10,000 units.

stories.headline: Plan emerges to bring passenger rail from Denver to Fort Collins by 2029 using existing funds
stories.summary:
Transportation planners are developing a 'Joint Service' proposal that would establish passenger rail service between Denver and Fort Collins as early as 2029 without requiring a voter-approved tax increase. The service would be funded through existing Denver RTD resources and fees already collected from car rentals and oil and gas extraction. Planned stops include Westminster, Broomfield, Louisville, Boulder, Longmont and Loveland.

Under the current proposal, a single diesel locomotive and passenger cars would make three round trips daily between the two cities, with the journey taking approximately 80 minutes each way. A second locomotive would be kept on standby for delays, accidents or maintenance. Track improvements from Westminster to Fort Collins, using BNSF railroad lines, are estimated to cost $333 million.

The Denver-Fort Collins corridor would build on RTD's existing commuter rail service north of Denver to Westminster. Proponents note that if the line proves successful, it could create political and economic pressure to extend service southward from Denver to Castle Rock, Colorado Springs and Pueblo. New Mexico has pursued a similar model with its regional Rail Runner service.

stories.headline: Denver weather turns cooler this weekend with scattered showers and thunderstorm chances
stories.summary:
Denver and the broader metro area can expect cooler temperatures this weekend, with spotty showers and isolated thunderstorms possible beginning Saturday afternoon, according to Fox31 Denver meteorologists. Winds out of the southeast at 5 to 15 mph will accompany the unsettled weather, though severe storms are not anticipated for the immediate metro area.

Rain chances increase substantially on Sunday, particularly for mountain communities and areas east of Denver. The high country and eastern plains are expected to see the most significant precipitation from the weekend system.

The cooler, wetter pattern offers some relief following days of elevated fire weather conditions that prompted red flag warnings across the Front Range and eastern plains earlier in the week.

stories.headline: Buc-ee's secures water well permits for potential Monument Hill location along I-25
stories.summary:
Documents from the Colorado Division of Water Resources show that the former owners of a parcel on Monument Hill — recently purchased by Buc-ee's affiliates — obtained permits to drill two commercial wells before the sale was completed. The two wells combined could yield up to approximately 33 acre-feet per year, or nearly 11 million gallons, intended for retail, restaurant, office and restroom uses at the planned travel center.

The Monument Hill site sits along Interstate 25 at the northern edge of El Paso County and has long been designated for a travel center development. Buc-ee's previously sought to annex the property into the nearby Town of Palmer Lake, proposing a collaborative water arrangement, but withdrew that annexation application in February after negotiations broke down.

Analysts note that the annual capacity of the newly permitted wells may fall short of the estimated daily demand projected in an earlier independently commissioned water report by roughly 2 million gallons per year. The first Colorado Buc-ee's location opened in Johnstown in 2024. No formal site development plans for the Monument Hill location have yet been submitted to El Paso County.

stories.headline: Colorado Sun wins 27 journalism honors, including top Public Service award, at regional competition
stories.summary:
The Colorado Sun claimed 27 total awards at the 2026 Top of the Rockies Excellence in Journalism competition, more first-place honors than any other news organization across the four-state region of Colorado, Utah, New Mexico and Wyoming. The outlet won the prestigious Public Service award for the second consecutive year, this time for its reporting on the impact of proposed Medicaid cuts, a project led by reporters John Ingold, Jesse Paul and Jennifer Brown.

The awards were announced Saturday night at a reception during the annual Colorado Professional Chapter of the Society of Professional Journalists convention in Denver. The Sun competed in the 'extra-large' newsroom category, defined as outlets with 15 or more staffers, and collected 13 first-place awards overall.

Individual staff members earned multiple honors. Reporter Jennifer Brown claimed three first-place awards covering health, arts and politics beats, while John Ingold won first place for science and technology reporting on cancer research. The Sun also received a first-place Solutions Journalism award for its ongoing 'Aging in Colorado' series examining challenges facing older residents in one of the nation's fastest-aging states.

stories.headline: Broncos complete NFL Draft with Day 3 additions focused on offensive playmakers
stories.summary:
The Denver Broncos wrapped up the 2026 NFL Draft on Saturday, using their remaining picks to add offensive weapons and depth on both lines. Denver opened Day 3 by selecting running back Jonah Coleman from Washington with the No. 108 overall pick, then took Boise State offensive lineman Kage Casey at No. 111. The team later traded up from No. 170 to No. 152 to select North Carolina State tight end Justin Joly, adding another tight end, Utah's Dallen Bentley, at pick No. 256.

The Broncos' final selection was Buffalo linebacker Red Murdock at No. 257 — the last pick in the entire draft, earning him the traditional 'Mr. Irrelevant' designation. Denver's offseason roster construction was headlined by the earlier trade for Miami wide receiver Jaylen Waddle, acquired in exchange for the No. 30 overall pick and a third-round selection.

General Manager George Paton said the team aimed to add youth to both offensive and defensive lines while increasing offensive explosion, particularly at running back and tight end. Coach Sean Payton called the draft class a strong complement to the team that reached the AFC Championship Game last season. Quarterback Bo Nix is expected to benefit from the new offensive weapons heading into the 2026 season.

stories.headline: Avalanche prepare to close out Kings series, seek fourth and final win in Los Angeles
stories.summary:
The Colorado Avalanche travel to Los Angeles on Sunday looking to close out their first-round NHL playoff series against the Kings, holding a commanding 3-0 series lead. The Avalanche have won each of the first three games, including a 4-2 victory in Game 3 earlier in the series.

Colorado will look to finish the series in four games, which would advance them to the second round of the Stanley Cup Playoffs. Analysts have identified securing the series clincher as a particular challenge, as teams protecting large leads sometimes struggle to close out opponents on the road.

The Kings have not won a game in the series and face elimination. A Colorado victory would end the series and give the Avalanche additional rest time before their next playoff opponent is determined.

stories.headline: Nuggets fall to Timberwolves again, now trail series 4-1 heading home
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets suffered another loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves on Friday, falling 112-96 to fall behind 4-1 in the first-round NBA playoff series. Center Nikola Jokic drew attention during the game when he lost his composure after Timberwolves forward Jaden McDaniels laughed at him during the lopsided defeat.

Nuggets head coach addressed criticism of the team's competitive spirit following the game, saying he believed Denver was competitive early before the performance deteriorated. The Nuggets are now one loss away from elimination in what is their eighth consecutive postseason appearance.

Denver returns home for Game 6 needing to win to extend the series. The Nuggets have not lost in the first round in recent playoff appearances, making the current deficit particularly notable for the franchise.

stories.headline: Aurora city councilmember arrested on DUI suspicion in January, details emerge
stories.summary:
Aurora City Councilmember Rob Andrews, who was elected to the council in November, was arrested on January 17 on suspicion of driving under the influence, according to the Denver Gazette. A breathalyzer test administered at the time of the arrest indicated Andrews had a measurable blood alcohol level, though specific figures were not detailed in initial reporting.

Andrews is a newly seated member of the Aurora City Council, having joined the body just weeks before the arrest occurred. The Aurora Police Department conducted the arrest. It is unclear whether Andrews faces formal charges or what the current status of any legal proceedings may be.

The disclosure comes as Aurora continues to navigate significant political scrutiny following multiple controversies involving local government and law enforcement in recent years. Aurora is the third-largest city in Colorado by population.

joke.joke: The first time you hold your newborn baby, you realize two things: I would do anything for this child, and I have no idea what I'm doing.

topStory.headline: Trump Evacuated Unhurt After Armed Suspect Charges Security at White House Correspondents' Dinner; One Officer Shot
topStory.summary:
President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump were evacuated Saturday evening from the White House Correspondents' Dinner at the Washington Hilton after an armed suspect charged a U.S. Secret Service security checkpoint at the venue. Hundreds of guests, including senior government officials, journalists, and Vice President JD Vance, took cover under tables in the ballroom as Secret Service agents with weapons drawn rushed personnel out of the room. The Secret Service confirmed that Trump and all protectees were unharmed.

The suspect was identified by the Associated Press and multiple outlets as Cole Tomas Allen, 31, of Torrance, California. Metropolitan Police Department interim chief Jeff Carroll said Allen was armed with a shotgun, a handgun, and multiple knives when he charged the checkpoint, and that investigators believe he fired at least one shot before being tackled to the ground and handcuffed. A Secret Service officer was struck by gunfire but was protected by a bullet-resistant vest and is expected to recover; Allen himself was not shot and was transported to a local hospital for evaluation. U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia Jeanine Pirro said Allen has been charged with felony firearms and assault offenses.

Trump later briefed reporters at the White House, saying he had spoken with the injured officer and praised the law enforcement response, comparing it favorably to the handling of the 2024 assassination attempt in Butler, Pennsylvania. He announced via Truth Social that the correspondents' dinner would be rescheduled within 30 days. CNN anchor Wolf Blitzer, who said he was within a few feet of the suspect when shots were fired, called into the network to describe witnessing Allen discharge what he described as a "very, very serious weapon."

World leaders issued statements condemning the incident and expressing relief that Trump and other guests were unharmed. Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, French President Emmanuel Macron, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, and Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi were among those who responded publicly. The incident is also prompting security reviews ahead of British King Charles III's scheduled state visit to the United States, set to begin approximately 24 hours after the shooting. Senior British government minister Darren Jones told Sky News that "extensive discussions" were underway with U.S. counterparts to assess any impact on operational planning for the visit.
